purpose: Electron main process entry point. Creates the BrowserWindow with persisted dimensions/position from settings, registers a custom 'app://' privileged protocol scheme (synchronously, BEFORE app.whenReady) for production asset loading from the project root / dist/ / public/ subdirectories, attaches preload.js to the renderer with contextIsolation enabled and webviewTag enabled (needed for the Live Preview tab), and wires app lifecycle: on whenReady it initializes settings, sets the macOS dock icon, registers all IPC handlers via registerIPCHandlers, creates the window, and bootstraps AutoUpdateService with a forwarding callback that pushes update events to the renderer. The dev/prod load split is determined by VITE_DEV_SERVER_URL env var: dev mode loads from Vite (HMR), production loads from app://synapse/dist/index.html. On window-all-closed, it stops watchers, disposes the auto-updater, and kills all spawned worker child processes via ClaudeCodeService.killAllWorkers() before app.quit() — without this, orphaned Claude CLI workers would survive past app exit.
gotchas: protocol.registerSchemesAsPrivileged MUST run synchronously at module load before app.whenReady — moving it inside whenReady breaks the app:// scheme registration
The app:// protocol handler tries PROJECT_ROOT first, then dist/, then public/ — adding a new asset directory requires editing the fallback chain in protocol.handle('app', ...)
webPreferences.webviewTag: true is required for the Live Preview tab's <webview> embedding — removing it silently breaks PreviewView.jsx
Window state is debounced with a 500ms timeout via saveWindowTimeout — calling settings.set on every resize/move event would thrash the JSON file
saveWindowState explicitly skips persistence when isMaximized() to preserve restore bounds (windowMaximized is tracked separately via the 'maximize'/'unmaximize' events)
killAllWorkers + stopWatchers + disposeAutoUpdater are wrapped in try/catch on quit — any throw would abort app.quit() and leave the process hanging

Renderer console messages are forwarded to main process stdout via webContents.on('console-message') — useful for debugging but means renderer console output appears in the terminal that launched npm start
patterns: Module-scoped mainWindow let with getMainWindow() accessor passed by reference to dependencies (registerIPCHandlers, AutoUpdateService) — allows late binding because the window may not exist when handlers are registered
Lazy require() inside lifecycle handlers (require('./settings'), require('./ipc-handlers')) so module load order doesn't trigger early instantiation
Debounced window state persistence (500ms) — settings writes are I/O and would otherwise hammer disk during drag-resize
Protocol fallback chain: direct path -> dist/ -> public/ — accommodates both Vite-built assets and legacy public/ files
Forwarding broadcast wrapper for AutoUpdateService: every emit checks win.isDestroyed() and webContents.isDestroyed() before send to avoid 'Object has been destroyed' errors during shutdown
conventions: Mac dock icon prefers 512x512 PNG over .icns (PNGs are more reliable in Electron's nativeImage)
backgroundColor: '#0a0a0c' set on BrowserWindow to avoid white flash before renderer paints
